_comment: Slice-groove listening aid: the GRID FALLBACK, which is this scenario's only reason to exist. Identical timeline to sampler_slice_drums.json, but the source is in_pad.wav -- sustained, low crest factor, no attacks -- so the SliceMap stays under kMinSlices (4) and STEP walks the tempo grid instead of transient markers. VERIFY, do not assume: the a_slices/b_slices column in the render CSV is the marker count, and it must read under 4 for the whole run. This scenario pointed at in_drone.wav until 2026-07-23, and the detector finds 24 onsets in that file inside this 1-9 s record window -- it ran in marker mode, identically to the drums scenario, and the fallback it claimed to demonstrate had no coverage at all. Record 1-9s, STEP from 10s. MOTION on a sampler deck is driven by MOD (set_depth), not the LANE_MOTION base (see part.cpp sampler MOTION note) -- opens 14/22/30; LANE_MOTION itself is pinned to 0 and made inactive in init (as sampler_solo.json does) so the ordered grid walk is actually ordered at the start -- Part's default base is 0.5, and leaving it there had the opening section half-scrambled while this comment promised order. DENS max from 26s raises the grain ceiling (it drove the roll dice too until rolls were removed, spec 2026-07-23). Action note: the plan draft named 'set_sampler_overlap', which is not in scenario.cpp's action table -- the nearest existing action is 'sampler_overlap' (inst.sampler_overlap(part, value)), used below.
